test.name: Browse a user-supplied image group without volume_search
test.description: When the user already has an imageGroupNumber, the skill should call image_search directly without first running volume_search, then log the browse. Tests that the volume-discovery step is correctly skipped when the group is known.

input.user_message: Browse FamilySearch image group 007936749 for me — list its images and log the browse so I can start looking for the Flynn family.

judge_context: The user supplied the imageGroupNumber directly. Calling image_search without volume_search is CORRECT here, not a gap — judges must not score partial for skipping volume_search when the group is already known.

CRITICAL HARNESS LIMITATION: page viewing is delegated to the image-reader subagent (@plugin:image-reader); the skill must NOT call image_read directly (it has no such tool). The subagent returns no image content in the harness (NOT READ), so the complete browse is: list the images and log the browse. Judges MUST NOT penalize the skill for not viewing pages or not finding a record.
image_search returns the whole volume in one call — the skill must not retry it with offset/limit/imageId parameters.
Should append a research_log_append entry recording the browse of image group 007936749.
